item.title: 🇺🇦💥 Ukraine Strikes Crimea in Large-Scale Attack Across Peninsula
item.description: Explosions reported across Russian-occupied Crimea as Ukraine launches apparent large-scale attack on the peninsula, targeting military infrastructure. - Kyiv Independent

item.title: 🇮🇷💧 US Strikes Hit Iranian Drinking Water Facility Near Hormuz
item.description: US military strikes near the Strait of Hormuz reportedly hit an Iranian drinking water facility, raising concerns about civilian infrastructure targeting. - Anadolu Agency

item.title: 🇯🇴⚔️ IRGC Claims Targeting US Fighter Jet Base and Military Facilities in Jordan
item.description: Iran Revolutionary Guard Corps says it targeted US military facilities including a fighter jet base in Jordan in retaliatory strikes. - Anadolu Agency

item.title: 🇮🇷⚽ Iran World Cup Fans Stay Excited for Football Despite War
item.description: Iran dedicated football supporters remain focused on World Cup despite ongoing conflict with US, showing resilience amid national crisis. - France 24

item.title: 🛢️🚢 Trump Says Over 100 Million Barrels of Oil Passed Through Hormuz
item.description: President Trump claims significant oil volumes continue through the strategic Strait of Hormuz despite Iranian threats to close the vital waterway. - Al Arabiya

item.title: 🇪🇺 European Central Bank Set for Insurance Rate Hike as Iran War Fans Inflation
item.description: The European Central Bank is all but certain to raise interest rates on Thursday in the hope of nipping higher inflation in the bud before a surge in energy costs triggered by the Iran war spreads more broadly across the euro zone economy. - Reuters

item.title: 🛢️ US Becomes Worlds Top Oil Exporter, Upending Decades-Old Global Energy Order
item.description: The United States has become the worlds largest oil exporter, overtaking Saudi Arabia and Russia in a historic shift that tightens American companies grip on energy markets as Washingtons war with Iran reshapes global energy trade. - Reuters

item.title: 🇸🇦 Saudi Arabia Lifts Five-Year Ban on Lebanese Imports in Support for Beirut Government
item.description: Saudi Arabia has lifted a roughly five-year ban on imports from Lebanon in a show of support for Beiruts embattled government, offering relief to businesses battered by years of conflict between Hezbollah and Israel. - Reuters

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israeli Airstrikes Kill 13 in Southern Lebanon as Hezbollah Campaign Continues
item.description: Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon killed at least 13 people on Wednesday, Lebanese security sources said, as Israel pressed its campaign against Hezbollah. The Iran-backed group claimed fresh attacks against Israeli forces in the south. - Reuters

item.title: ⚔️ US and Iran Trade Air Attacks for Second Day as Ceasefire Unravels
item.description: The United States and Iran exchanged air attacks for a second straight day, with President Trump vowing further strikes if Tehran does not immediately agree to a peace deal. The renewed fighting has cast fresh doubt over prospects for ending the conflict. - Reuters

item.title: ⚔️ Iran and US exchange fire as hostilities escalate across Gulf
item.description: Iran retaliated against US strikes with attacks on Kuwait and Bahrain, while the US military announced it completed additional strikes on multiple Iranian targets. Bahrain activated missile warning sirens and Kuwait temporarily closed its airspace. - AP News

item.title: 💰 Dollar wobbles as Middle East strikes unsettle markets
item.description: The US dollar fluctuated on Thursday as fresh US military strikes in the Middle East undermined sentiment, while a surge in May consumer inflation to a three-year high kept investors uneasy about the Federal Reserve outlook. - Reuters

item.title: 🇦🇲 Trump praises Armenia PM election win despite Russian pressure
item.description: US President Donald Trump congratulated Armenian Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan on his Civil Contract party decisive victory in weekend elections, noting the win came despite significant Russian pressure on the country. - Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine strikes Russian-occupied Mariupol port
item.description: Ukrainian forces struck the Russian-occupied port of Mariupol, Kyiv said on Wednesday, the latest in a series of drone attacks on logistics across a critical stretch of Moscow-held southern Ukraine connecting Russia to Crimea. - Reuters

item.title: 🛢️ Iran threatens to target any ship in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran military command announced it will target any vessel transiting the Strait of Hormuz after striking two vessels in the strategic waterway, marking a significant escalation in tensions with the United States. - France24

item.title: 🇪🇺📊 EU fiscal watchdog criticises loosening budget rules for energy transition
item.description: The European Fiscal Board warned that allowing governments to spend defence fiscal flexibility on clean energy transition undermines EU fiscal discipline, amid debates over how to fund both priorities. - Reuters

item.title: 🇮🇳📉 Indian shares set to open lower on Middle East escalation and US inflation
item.description: Indian equities are likely to open lower as fresh escalation between the US and Iran and a sharp jump in US inflation trigger risk-off sentiment across global markets. - Reuters

item.title: ⛪🕊️ Pope Leo XIV blesses Gaudis Sagrada Familia on centenary of architects death
item.description: Pope Leo XIV celebrated Mass at Barcelonas iconic Sagrada Familia basilica exactly 100 years after architect Antoni Gaudis death, blessing the towering architectural masterpiece. - CNN

item.title: 🇪🇺 Five EU countries call for united response to Trump drug price threats
item.description: Five EU member states are pushing for a coordinated European response to US pressure on pharmaceutical pricing, warning against bilateral deals that could undermine the bloc. - Politico

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK-EU summit at risk as youth visa talks hit fresh obstacle
item.description: A planned UK-EU summit faces delays as negotiations over youth mobility visas stall, with both sides struggling to find common ground on migration terms. - Politico

item.title: 🇮🇳 India calls in air force to secure exam papers after scandal fuels protests
item.description: The Indian Air Force has been tasked with transporting and securing examination papers after cheating scandals sparked nationwide protests over exam integrity. - SCMP

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 US Strikes Multiple Targets in Iran as Trump Vows Further Escalation
item.description: The US military launched new strikes against multiple targets in Iran overnight, with President Donald Trump threatening additional attacks if no peace deal is reached. Iran announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz in response. - Reuters

item.title: 🇬🇧 Police Fire Water Cannons at Belfast Protesters as Unrest Flares After Stabbing
item.description: Northern Ireland saw renewed violence as police used water cannons against protesters in Belfast following a stabbing incident. A Sudanese man appeared in court charged with attempted murder. - AP News

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump White House UFC Fight Requires $60M and 7 Federal Agencies
item.description: President Donald Trump's planned UFC fight on the White House South Lawn requires a monumental effort from more than seven federal agencies and an estimated $60 million in costs. - AP News

item.title: 🇯🇵⚡ Japan Restarts Reactor at World's Largest Nuclear Plant Amid Energy Push
item.description: A reactor at the world's largest nuclear power plant in Japan has resumed operations as the country pushes to maximize nuclear power generation to cope with growing electricity needs and climate goals. - AP News
